sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: CHF29.95 DBK.SW Deutsche Bank (SIX) pre-market: oversold bounce watch CHF31.50
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank AG (DBK.SW) is trading at CHF29.95 pre-market on SIX, presenting an oversold bounce opportunity with a tactical target of CHF31.50. Meyka AI rates DBK.SW a B (HOLD) with a one-year projection of CHF34.65, despite current low liquidity and upcoming earnings on January 29, 2026. Investors are advised to use tight risk controls and limit orders for this trade.

feed.title: Assessing S&P Global (SPGI) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum
feed.summary: S&P Global (SPGI) has seen significant share price momentum recently, with an 8.2% gain in the last month and 12.3% in the past three months. However, the article argues that SPGI's current valuation, with a Price-to-Earnings ratio of 38.9x and a discounted cash flow (DCF) analysis, suggests the stock is overvalued compared to its peers and estimated fair value. Investors are encouraged to consider the company's business quality against these valuation metrics.

feed.title: Expedia Powers Communications with CEDAR Audio’s VoicEX 2
feed.summary: Expedia Group is utilizing CEDAR Audio's VoicEX 2 plugin to enhance the audio quality of its internal communications, especially during livestreamed "town hall" meetings. The AI-powered tool effectively removes background noise and suppresses reverb, allowing Expedia's AV Production Engineer Matt Jones to maintain high-quality, professional-sounding speech for the global workforce. VoicEX 2 has become an essential part of Expedia's production toolkit due to its reliable performance and ease of use.

feed.title: Virginia slot routes surge in December
feed.summary: Revenue from historical horse racing (HHR) machines in Virginia increased by 10.7 percent in December, with Churchill Downs grossing $48.7 million from these machines. J.P. Morgan analyst Daniel Politzer noted that the average win per machine per day was down 5.5 percent, partially attributed to adverse winter weather, which is slightly worse than typical seasonality. The Rose in Prince William was the largest contributor, generating $13.9 million, while Rosie’s in Emporia achieved the state's best per-slot daily yield at $445.

feed.title: KEPCO to join hands with U.S. city to develop power distribution grid solutions
feed.summary: Korea Electric Power Corp. (KEPCO) has signed an MOU with Columbia, a U.S. city in South Carolina, to develop and test power distribution grid solutions customized for the American market. KEPCO will implement its advanced distribution management and building energy management systems in Columbia, which aims to power its grid entirely with renewable energy by 2036. This collaboration is intended to help KEPCO expand its presence in the U.S. power market.

feed.title: Analysts Optimistic on Devon Energy’s (DVN) 2026 Production Outlook
feed.summary: Analysts are largely optimistic about Devon Energy's (DVN) 2026 production outlook, with Barclays maintaining a Hold rating and a $40 price target, and Bernstein reiterating a Buy rating with a revised $42 price target due to anticipated short-term oil price pressure. Roth Capital also forecasts 2026 production to slightly exceed expectations, highlighting the company's compelling valuation and strong free cash flow generation, despite expected declines in Bakken assets.

feed.title: How Investors May Respond To CyberArk (CYBR) Spotlighting Just‑in‑Time Privileged Access Gaps In AI Era
feed.summary: CyberArk's recent research indicates that only 1% of organizations have fully implemented Just-in-Time privileged access, with 91% still using always-on access, highlighting security gaps amplified by AI. This reinforces CyberArk's investment narrative around identity security, particularly with its new Secure AI Agents Solution, aiming to expand its role from a point solution to a broad identity security provider. While the company projects significant revenue and earnings growth, execution risks related to recent acquisitions remain a factor for investors to consider.

feed.title: The Truth About Tapestry Inc.: Is This Fashion Stock a Sneaky Steal or an Overhyped Trap?
feed.summary: The article analyzes whether Tapestry Inc. (TPR), the parent company of Coach, Kate Spade, and Stuart Weitzman, is a worthwhile investment. It highlights the company's "attainable luxury" positioning, brand relevance on platforms like TikTok, and a potentially underrated stock performance with a dividend, contrasting it with flashier luxury brands and its competitor, Capri Holdings. The verdict suggests Tapestry could be a "sneaky steal" for long-term investors seeking stability and dividend potential, rather than an overhyped trap.

feed.title: Google Shares Jump After Hours on Apple Deal to Integrate Gemini With Siri
feed.summary: Google's parent company, Alphabet Inc. (GOOG), saw its shares jump after Apple announced a deal to integrate Google's Gemini AI into Siri as part of its Apple Intelligence platform. This partnership positions Gemini as a core engine for Siri, potentially boosting Alphabet's valuation and AI revenue growth, despite competitive pressures from OpenAI. The market reacted positively, while some, like Elon Musk, raised concerns about power concentration.

feed.title: Hear Enova break down its 2025 performance on Jan. 27 earnings call
feed.summary: Enova (NYSE: ENVA) will release its fourth quarter and full-year 2025 financial results after market close on Tuesday, January 27, 2026. The company will host a conference call on the same day at 4:00 p.m. Central Time / 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time to discuss these results. A live webcast will be available, and a replay will be accessible until February 3, 2026.

feed.title: BWXT to answer six pages of community questions, commissioners delay rezoning vote
feed.summary: Washington County, Tennessee commissioners have delayed a vote on a controversial rezoning proposal from BWXT until February 25 at the company's request. BWXT plans to answer six pages of community questions regarding its proposed expansion in Jonesborough to create high purity depleted uranium. Residents, organized by "Protect Jonesborough," have raised concerns about the project's impact on the watershed and potential for accidents.

feed.title: Synopsys GC Janet Lee sells $132k in shares
feed.summary: Janet Lee, General Counsel & Corporate Secretary at Synopsys Inc (NASDAQ:SNPS), sold 250 shares for $132,500 on January 12, 2026, reducing her direct ownership to 11,959 shares. This comes as Synopsys reported strong fourth-quarter revenues, with KeyBanc, Morgan Stanley, BofA Securities, and Rosenblatt Securities all raising price targets due to positive outlooks and growth drivers, despite some challenges in IP and China revenue. Wells Fargo, however, adjusted its price target lower due to mixed guidance on Core EDA growth.

feed.title: Shailesh Jejurikar is P&G's new CEO | People to Watch
feed.summary: Shailesh Jejurikar has been appointed as the new CEO of Procter & Gamble, becoming the first Asian to lead the company. With a long career in P&G's fabric care division, Jejurikar steps into the role amidst a two-year restructuring plan that includes cutting 7,000 non-manufacturing jobs due to slowing global sales. This move positions him to potentially see a significant increase in his personal stake if the company's stock rises.

feed.title: Why the Market Dipped But NRG Energy (NRG) Gained Today
feed.summary: NRG Energy (NRG) shares rose +1.14% against a declining market, outperforming the S&P 500's 0.19% loss. Despite recent monthly losses and a 'Sell' rating from Zacks, analysts anticipate a significant year-over-year revenue increase in its upcoming earnings report. The company also shows a favorable valuation when compared to its industry average.

feed.title: Why the Market Dipped But Garmin (GRMN) Gained Today
feed.summary: Garmin (GRMN) saw a gain of +1.14% in the latest trading session, outperforming the broader market which experienced a dip. The company's upcoming earnings report is expected to show a slight decrease in EPS but a significant increase in revenue year-over-year. Garmin currently holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold) and is trading at a slight discount to its industry average in terms of Forward P/E ratio.

feed.title: Booking Holdings (BKNG) Dips More Than Broader Market: What You Should Know
feed.summary: Booking Holdings (BKNG) experienced a 1.42% dip in its latest trading session, underperforming the broader S&P 500. Investors are keenly anticipating its upcoming earnings release, with EPS expected to grow by 16.97% and revenue by 11.95% from the prior-year quarter. The company currently holds a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy) and its valuation metrics, including a Forward P/E of 20.47 and PEG ratio of 1.17, suggest suitability for growth-oriented investors.

feed.title: Enbridge (ENB) Rises As Market Takes a Dip: Key Facts
feed.summary: Enbridge (ENB) experienced a 1.05% gain at market close, outperforming the S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq, which all saw losses. The oil and natural gas transportation company is set to announce its earnings on February 13, 2026, with projected EPS of $0.57, a 7.55% increase year-over-year. Analysts' positive revisions to forecasts reflect confidence in Enbridge's business performance, contributing to its current Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).

feed.title: GECC Reports F2026 Q1 Financial Results
feed.summary: Global Education Communities Corp. (GECC) has released its Q1 F2026 financial results, showing significant improvement with a 114% increase in net income from continuing operations compared to the previous year. This positive performance is largely attributed to the company's strategic shift to focus on student housing after divesting a major part of its educational division. GECC also reported substantial progress on its student housing developments in Vancouver, including GEC Oakridge, GEC Education Mega Center (Surrey), and GEC Langara.

feed.title: Darling Ingredients Turns Waste Into Cash, But Margins Are Thin
feed.summary: Darling Ingredients (NYSE: DAR) transforms animal and food waste into valuable ingredients for feed, food, and fuel. Despite strong free cash flow and growth prospects in renewable fuels and specialty proteins, the company faces challenges including a premium valuation, high leverage, thin margins, and policy risks. Investors are looking to the upcoming Q4 2025 earnings report and the closing of its Nextida collagen joint venture for future insights.

feed.title: Arden Launches Heated Cushion Seat and Partners With USA Curling to Support Athletes’ Families
feed.summary: Arden, a leading outdoor furnishings brand, has partnered with USA Curling to launch a new heated, portable stadium cushion and provide travel assistance to families of athletes Korey Dropkin and Cory Thiesse. This initiative aims to enhance comfort for fans in cold-weather sporting environments and enable families to support their athletes on the global stage. The partnership underscores the importance of family and community in the sport of curling, offering both product innovation and emotional support.

feed.title: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. Stock Underperforms Tuesday When Compared To Competitors
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NCLH) stock underperformed on Tuesday, falling 2.14%. The article also notes that Unity Software (U.US) reported strong fourth-quarter financial results with revenue of $609 million, exceeding estimates, despite a quarterly loss of 66 cents per share.

feed.title: BE Stock Surges 36% With A 5-day Winning Spree On AEP Deal
feed.summary: The article reports that BE Stock has surged by 36% and is currently on a 5-day winning spree. This significant rise in stock value is attributed to a deal involving AEP.

feed.title: Tech Bytes: RAMageddon isn’t over — and Micron says it’s a multi-year problem
feed.summary: Micron Technology Inc. has warned that the global supply of DRAM (dynamic random access memory) is unlikely to improve significantly before 2028, leading to a multi-year problem for device makers. This shortage is attributed to rising DRAM prices, tightening inventories, and a significant shift in production priorities towards AI-linked products due to intense demand from AI infrastructure. The sustained high demand from AI, less sensitive to economic swings, suggests that elevated memory pricing will be a long-term feature rather than a temporary phase, impacting hardware costs across consumer devices.

feed.title: CoreWeave, Inc. Securities Fraud Class Action Result of Undisclosed Deployment Issues and Stock Decline - Investors may Contact Lewis Kahn, Esq, at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC
feed.summary: Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC (KSF) is reminding investors of CoreWeave, Inc. (NasdaqGS: CRWV) who incurred substantial losses that they have until March 13, 2026, to file lead plaintiff applications in a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that CoreWeave and its executives made false and misleading statements regarding the company's ability to meet customer demand and understated risks associated with reliance on a single third-party data center supplier. Investors who purchased CoreWeave securities between March 28, 2025, and December 15, 2025, are affected.

feed.title: HUTCHMED Highlights Publication of Phase III SACHI Results in The Lancet
feed.summary: HUTCHMED (China) Limited announced the publication of its Phase III SACHI trial results in The Lancet. The study demonstrated the effectiveness of the savolitinib and osimertinib combination in treating advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) with MET amplification after progression on prior EGFR-TKI treatment. This combination has already received regulatory approval in China in June 2025.

feed.title: Inside Cimpress' $700M merch push tying VistaPrint to sister brands
feed.summary: Cimpress plc announced a strategic move to integrate VistaPrint with sister brands National Pen and BuildASign to enhance its "elevated products" offering, including promotional items, apparel, signage, and packaging. This collaboration aims to leverage shared capabilities and accelerate revenue growth, particularly in the promotional products category, which already generates $700 million annually. The company also revealed a leadership transition at National Pen, with Bryan Kranik succeeding Peter Kelly as CEO while retaining his role at BuildASign, aiming for $600 million in adjusted EBITDA and 4-6% constant currency revenue growth by FY2028.

feed.title: Is Revvity (RVTY) Offering Value After Recent Share Price Rebound?
feed.summary: This article analyzes whether Revvity (RVTY) is attractively priced after a recent share price rebound. Using a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model, Simply Wall St suggests Revvity is undervalued by 14.2%, with an estimated intrinsic value of $128.43 per share compared to its recent price of $110.14. However, its P/E ratio of 53.71x is significantly higher than Simply Wall St's "Fair Ratio" of 25.38x, indicating it might be overvalued based on earnings.

feed.title: Ford announces multi-year partnership with Carhartt to launch Super Duty truck, other initiatives
feed.summary: Ford Motor Company and Carhartt have announced a multi-year partnership to launch a special edition Super Duty truck and implement community initiatives aimed at attracting skilled tradespeople. This collaboration seeks to address workforce development, community building, and durable products under Ford's Essential Economy initiative, including partnering with ToolBank USA to provide tools for trade education and disaster relief. The partnership also includes new co-branded merchandise and a 2027 Super Duty Carhartt truck, with design inspirations drawn from Carhartt's Detroit store.

feed.title: Health pivot and cash crunch define Visionary Holdings’ 2025 results
feed.summary: Visionary Holdings (Nasdaq: GV) released its final annual report for fiscal year 2025, showing a significant 46.2% decrease in revenue to $5.04M and a net loss of $15.75M. The company is actively transitioning from education and real estate to health management, anti-aging, and medical aesthetics, with emerging health businesses contributing $1.06M to revenue. Visionary Holdings is addressing substantial financial pressures including $54.50M in negative working capital through equity financing and creditor negotiations while pending its Form 20-F filing with the SEC.

feed.title: Belden prices €450 million senior subordinated notes at 4.25% By Investing.com
feed.summary: Belden Inc. announced the pricing of €450 million in 4.250% senior subordinated notes due 2033 at 100% of their principal amount, with the offering expected to close on January 28, 2026. The company plans to use the net proceeds, along with cash on hand, to redeem its existing 3.375% senior subordinated notes due 2027 by February 11, 2026. This move aligns with Belden's strategic financial restructuring, highlighted by its solid financial position, recent strong Q3 2025 earnings, and new leadership appointments, despite a current market valuation suggesting it is slightly overvalued.

feed.title: Is Dynex Capital (DX) Pricing Look Sustainable After 1 Year Return Of 33.6%?
feed.summary: This article analyzes the valuation of Dynex Capital (DX) after a 33.6% return over the last year. Using an Excess Returns model, the stock appears 52.7% overvalued, while a Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio comparison suggests it is undervalued. The article emphasizes that different valuation methods can lead to varied conclusions, highlighting the importance of considering multiple perspectives and narrative-based assessments.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan Stock: Huge Breakout. Now What? (NYSE:FCX)
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) has reached new 15-year highs as copper prices hit all-time records, driven by favorable tariffs and a significant U.S. copper price premium, providing a $1.7 billion annual benefit. Despite production setbacks in Indonesia and lower 2025–2026 output limiting near-term revenue growth, the stock is rated a hold, with expectations for consolidation around $60–64. Future EPS growth is anticipated from leach initiatives, though not enough to cause another breakout in 2026.

feed.title: Is It Time To Reassess Honeywell (HON) After Recent Share Price Strength?
feed.summary: Honeywell International's (HON) stock has seen recent strength, returning 5.4% over 7 days and 7.4% year-to-date. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 15.4% at its current price of US$210.28, with an estimated intrinsic value of $248.66 per share. The company's P/E ratio of 21.78x is above the sector average but below its peer group, and a proprietary "Fair Ratio" also indicates it's trading below its estimated normal earnings multiple.

feed.title: Home Repair and Care program launched to address energy costs
feed.summary: A new "Home Repair and Care" program has been launched in Henderson, Kentucky, to help CenterPoint Energy customers and southwestern Indiana residents. This program, a partnership between Community One and the CenterPoint Energy Foundation, aims to reduce energy waste and improve efficiency through home repairs. Funding comes from the $5 million Community Energy Improvement Fund.

feed.title: Is It Time To Reconsider NetApp (NTAP) After Recent Share Price Weakness?
feed.summary: NetApp (NTAP) has experienced recent share price weakness, with a 7.2% decline over the last 30 days, despite mixed long-term returns. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is significantly undervalued, estimating its intrinsic value at $185.91 per share against a current price of $107.28. The company's P/E ratio of 18.1x also indicates it is undervalued compared to a calculated "Fair Ratio" of 25.1x.

feed.title: Has The Recent Rally Left Wells Fargo (WFC) Trading Above Its Regulatory Rebuild Progress?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Wells Fargo's (WFC) valuation against its recent stock performance and ongoing regulatory rebuild. Using an Excess Returns model, it suggests WFC is undervalued by 23.7%, while a P/E analysis indicates it trades above its industry average but below Simply Wall St's proprietary Fair Ratio. The article also presents bull and bear case narratives for the stock, with fair value estimates ranging from US$74.70 to US$100.00.

feed.title: Weaviate named a Leader and Outperformer by GigaOm and Emerging Leader by Gartner; Market Momentum Accelerates as Nonrelational DBMS Segment Grows 22.7%
feed.summary: The article discusses how Weaviate has been recognized as a Leader and Outperformer by GigaOm and an Emerging Leader by Gartner. This recognition highlights Weaviate's accelerating market momentum, coinciding with a significant 22.7% growth in the nonrelational DBMS segment.

feed.title: BioNTech’s Strategic Pivot: Funding an Oncology Ambition with Cash Reserves
feed.summary: BioNTech, known for its COVID-19 vaccine, is aggressively shifting its focus to oncology, backed by €17.2 billion in cash reserves. The company plans to initiate six new Phase 3 clinical studies and deliver seven key data readouts in 2026, transforming into a diversified oncology leader despite an anticipated decline in Comirnaty vaccine revenues. This strategic pivot is seen as crucial for its post-COVID future, though the market has reacted cautiously, with the stock declining 15% over the last year.

feed.title: MGM launches invite-only rewards program for non-gaming guests
authors: David Danzis and Richard N. Velotta
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International has launched "The Fountain Club by MGM," an invite-only rewards program for its high-value, non-gaming guests. The program offers personalized concierge services, exclusive event invitations, and priority reservations, among other luxury perks. Selected guests received invitations late last year, with more expected to be sent out this year.

feed.title: VICI Properties Stock: A Bargain In The REIT Market (NYSE:VICI)
feed.summary: VICI Properties has been upgraded to a Strong Buy due to its compelling yield, historically low valuation despite record revenues and raised guidance. The recent Golden Portfolio acquisition further diversifies cash flows and adds Las Vegas Locals exposure with attractive cap rates and built-in escalators. While risks from tenant concentration and macro headwinds exist, VICI's triple-net model, CPI-linked escalators, and land holdings support continued growth and accretive deals.

feed.title: Lee County begins countywide conversion to Tyler Technologies; probation, courts already migrated
feed.summary: Lee County, Illinois, is undergoing a countywide data conversion to Tyler Technologies, with probation and court services already fully migrated to the new system since December 1st. Staff training is ongoing, and a courthouse kickoff event is scheduled for January 21st with Tyler representatives. While most of the system is operational, officials are still working on establishing external connections and collaborations for full integration.

feed.title: AMC.AX Amcor plc ASX hits A$64.10 on heavy volume 14 Jan 2026: Watch liquidity
feed.summary: Amcor plc (AMC.AX) experienced significant intraday trading on January 14, 2026, with its stock price reaching A$64.10 on heavy volume, up from a prior close of A$12.95. This surge is attributed to trading momentum ahead of upcoming earnings on February 3, 2026, and increased speculative activity in the sector. While Meyka AI rates AMC.AX as a "HOLD" with a score of 63.92/100, its model projects a potential downside of 85.92% to A$9.02, contrasting with analyst targets ranging from A$50.00 to A$85.00.

feed.title: Bill Ford: $20 billion EV pivot about ‘recognizing what reality was’
feed.summary: Ford CEO Jim Farley stated that the company is not moving backward on electric vehicles, while Bill Ford commented that the $20 billion EV pivot was about "recognizing what reality was." The article also mentions the F-150 Lightning and its production ending in late 2025.

feed.title: Intel Corporation(INTC) KeyCorp Upgrades to Overweight from Sector Weight
feed.summary: KeyCorp upgraded Intel Corporation (INTC) to Overweight from Sector Weight, setting a 12-month price target of $60.00. This upgrade signals increased confidence in Intel's turnaround strategy, particularly its manufacturing roadmap, IDM 2.0 model, and improving market conditions in PC and data center sectors. The firm also highlighted Intel's improving competitive dynamics, cost discipline, and the support of government incentives for domestic semiconductor manufacturing.

feed.title: Are Sysco's U.S. Foodservice Operations Gaining Executional Traction?
feed.summary: Sysco's U.S. Foodservice business demonstrated improved execution in the first quarter of fiscal 2026, driven by better operational performance and sequential growth in local volume trends despite mixed overall demand. The company reported a 2.9% year-over-year sales increase to $14.8 billion, with total U.S. Foodservice case volume up 0.1%, and local volumes showing improvement after accounting for a business exit. These gains, including positive local volume growth in U.S. Broadline, reflect enhanced customer service levels and supply-chain efficiency.

feed.title: Is TripAdvisor (TRIP) Stock Undervalued Right Now?
feed.summary: This article analyzes TripAdvisor (TRIP) stock using Zacks' Style Scores system, specifically focusing on its "Value" category. It highlights TRIP's Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) and an "A" Value grade, supported by favorable P/E, PEG, and P/B ratios compared to its industry. The analysis suggests that TRIP is currently undervalued and offers a strong outlook based on its earnings potential.

feed.title: Will Boot Barn (BOOT) Beat Estimates Again in Its Next Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Boot Barn (BOOT) is showing strong potential to beat its next earnings estimates, building on a history of exceeding expectations in previous quarters. With a positive Zacks Earnings ESP of +3.98% and a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), the company is well-positioned for another positive surprise. Its next earnings report is anticipated on February 4, 2026.

feed.title: Cheche Group Announces Receipt of Notification Letter from Nasdaq
feed.summary: Cheche Group Inc. (NASDAQ: CCG) announced receipt of a notification letter from Nasdaq indicating non-compliance with the minimum bid price requirement, as its Class A ordinary shares have traded below US$1.00 for 30 consecutive business days. The company has a 180-calendar-day compliance period, until July 13, 2026, to regain compliance, with no immediate impact on its listing or business operations. Cheche Group intends to monitor its share price and explore options to meet the Nasdaq requirement.

feed.title: Cogent Biosciences Announces Anticipated 2026 Commercial and Clinical Milestones for Bezuclastinib and Precision Therapies Portfolio
feed.summary: Cogent Biosciences has announced its anticipated commercial and clinical milestones for 2026, focusing on bezuclastinib and its precision therapies portfolio. Key plans include submitting New Drug Applications (NDAs) for bezuclastinib in multiple indications like NonAdvSM, AdvSM, and GIST, with potential commercial launch in the second half of 2026. The company also expects to submit Investigational New Drug (IND) applications for novel pan-KRAS(ON) and JAK2 V617F inhibitors, supported by a strong financial position of approximately $900 million.

feed.title: PTx Winter Conference Highlights Practical Ag Innovations
feed.summary: AGCO's PTx division is hosting its first annual Winter Conference under the PTx brand in Tremont, Illinois, from January 20-23, 2026. This premier event will showcase next-generation precision agriculture technologies, including water management, autonomy planting, and spraying innovations, aiming to provide practical solutions and educational insights for farmers, media, and investors. The conference will also feature exclusive research from Precision Planting's PTI Farm and integrate PTx technology with Fendt products in interactive sessions.
